title: Consumer rights — unfair trade practices, product liability and higher commissions
text: Under the Consumer Protection Act 2019, a consumer has the right to approach the State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission for complaints where the value exceeds one crore rupees but does not exceed ten crore rupees. The National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission handles complaints exceeding ten crore rupees. The Act introduces product liability under Chapter VI — a product manufacturer, product service provider, or product seller shall be liable in a product liability action if the product contains a manufacturing defect, design defect, deviation from manufacturing specifications, fails to conform to express warranty, or if the product does not contain adequate instructions for correct use. A consumer who suffers harm due to a defective product can claim compensation without proving negligence — strict liability applies. Under Section 18, the Central Consumer Protection Authority has the power to investigate unfair trade practices, false and misleading advertisements, and violations of consumer rights. The Authority can impose penalties on manufacturers and advertisers who make false claims. A consumer who has been cheated by a false advertisement, received counterfeit goods, been charged hidden fees, or been denied a legitimate warranty claim has the right to file a complaint under this Act. The limitation period for filing a consumer complaint is two years from the date of cause of action.
